claim.label: Nationalist Emergence
claim.query: volunteer movement patriotic nation gaelic duty sacrifice republican
claim.counter_query: foreign travel commerce workers minority plural civic
claim.thesis: Surface the years and documents where nationalist discipline becomes a strong semantic organizing force.

Mr. ‘Redmend speaks of the next generation.

Now, there are three kinds of jobs, and likewise three

kinds of job-getters. You have (1) Jobs obtained by

competitive examination ; (2) Neutral positions, such as

educational ones, or that the late Dr. Coyne had, and

(8) ‘Political governing jobs. And, as for job-getters,

you ‘have (1) Examination competitors; (2) Neutral

personages, who mind their own ‘business; (3) Political

personages. Now, as to the.combination of the third

in each, or even the third with the second, we are all

ing situation in which it was sought to place them.

That sounds very well if one could forget for the

moment the organ that was blowing the tune. So the

‘“ Ha’penny Dreadful’’ chides people for deviating

from the “bold path!”  Mr. Murphy, in a Robert

Emmet costume, is rather comic, certainly!

But what next for the ‘“ Independent?’ Now that

do? As far as we can see it does not propose to de

contradictory_documents.title: THE LEADER, VOL. XIV NO. 19, JUNE 29 1907
contradictory_documents.chunk_text:
exertion. The old proverb that necessity has no law, is,

in a way, exemplified on the barren hills of Kerry,

where the people toil, whilst on the Meath prairie

nature is allowed to do mnearly all the work.' At

Williamstown, on our’way back, Mr. Anthony Scott

took us into the house of Mr. Finnegan, a big farmer,

as farming is understood in Meath. Mr. Finuegan is

seventy years of age, a big kindly man, who ought to

know Meath if anyone knows it. ‘‘ It is not the land

for the people we want here,”” said he, “ but the people

for the land.”” Supported by such mature and weighty

authority, we do not mind hazarding the view that that

‘sums up the situation in rural Meath where Ireland is

not at work. There is not much prospect of Meath

contradictory_documents.chunk_text:
Fonritng is the name of a new system of shorthand

just published by Messrs. Sealy, Bryers and Walker,

and paper, printing and engraving are Irish, as is also

the author who conceals his identity under the initials

R. M. R. The book is probably the first of its kind

published in Dublin.  The author claims that

\vithin a month, by ordinary diligence, any one should

be able to write it as quickly as ordinary longhand;

and in from three to six months, according to individual

talent and the amount of exercise, it is possible to

acquire sufficient speed to report an ordinary speaker.’””

A well-known man in the Irish Language Movement is

engaged in adopting Fonritng to Irish.

Athenry has had notoriety thrust upon it. During

recent times some wanton fellows spiked a meadow of

wooden pavement. It did not require the use of a viagraph to

show them how the first portion was put down. If the members

of the Corporation only put the'r heads together, there would be

ths finest picce of wooden pavement, in Great Britain or Ireland.

But there was something worse than mere incapacity. Our city

had achieved a fame very much resembling infamy.

The preacher, who evidently looks to the East where

England ‘ies, for England’s good opinion, also said :—

I our Lord on beholding the city of Jerusalem from the Mount

all was not hopeless or lost. Something st’ll remained to be

letter of Mr. John Redmond he writes—* Of course, all
we ask is autonomy in purely Irish affairs.” This seems
to me to give up the right of nationhood for the Irish

has a right to do this. It is inherent in the nation and
cannot be resigned to a foreign power.”” No Irishman,

contextual_chunks.text:
30

The country had better keep a sharp eye on the
 ZIndependent. It has already, with the help of the
- Irish Times and other designing factors, inflicted Hum-
‘phrey’s Dump (Failed) on this country, and the unfor-
tunate guarantors have a simply deplorable prospect to
face. The Zudependent would like the people to forget
that it forced this calamity on Ireland. In a recent
report it suppressed the reference of Mr. Orr, a director
of Messrs. Thwaites & Co., to the Exhibition. It was at
its suppressing game again in connection with the sum-
mons re the Nigger minstrels at the late Exhibition.
Mr. William Gibson, who appeared for the coloured
lady, in the course of his statement as reported in the
Irish- Temes, said that “ Mrs. Roberts was the pro-
prietress of a negro threatrical troupe of ten or more
artistes, and that her agent was a Mr. Gannon, of
Bristol. In October last she was engaged by TFisher
and a Mr. Travers through Gannon to come over here
and perform at the International Exhibition. The Con-
cert Department of the Exhibition was under the direc-
tion of Mr. McGuckin, amongst whose paid licutenants
was Travers, who, it appeared, had associated him
